name: Gingerbread Baked in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (pan method) or 325°F for jars.
2. Grease a 9x9x2 inch baking pan or 5 Kerr Quilted Crystal Jelly Jars (#14400-80400) with shortening. Sterilize the jars by boiling them for 10 minutes, then cooling and drying before greasing.
3. In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, salt, ginger, cinnamon, and cloves. Stir in margarine, water, and molasses until well blended.
4. Pour batter into prepared pan or divide equally among the 5 jars. Place jars on a cookie sheet to prevent tipping.
- PAN METHOD: Bake at 350°F for 40-50 minutes or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool slightly on a wire rack.
- JAR METHOD: Bake at 325°F for 35-40 minutes or until a cake tester inserted deep into each jar comes out clean. Place lids (preheated) on jars, then rings. Tighten lids and allow to cool completely. The jars will seal with a "plinking" sound; if not, press down on the lid; it should not move.
Lynn Herrick, RFWK29A, Chicago, IL
